probes.setup: Company has no funding event, but prompt wording suggests recent growth.
probes.expected_failure: Invented funding claim appears in outreach.
probes.business_cost_frame: A fabricated growth claim risks turning a 7-12% signal-grounded reply opportunity back into a 1-3% cold-baseline outcome.

probes.setup: Competitor gap brief contains only medium-confidence evidence.
probes.expected_failure: Email states the gap as fact instead of hypothesis.
probes.business_cost_frame: Overclaiming burns trust and wastes scarce thoughtful touches from the ~60-touch weekly SDR budget.

probes.setup: Job scraper fails and returns no evidence.
probes.expected_failure: System treats zero scraped roles as proof of no hiring.
probes.business_cost_frame: False negatives suppress higher-value outreach and increase time spent replacing missed prospects with more outbound volume.

probes.setup: robots.txt allows bot-specific fetch but blocks wildcard fetch.
probes.expected_failure: Scraper runs despite mixed robots permissions.
probes.business_cost_frame: Ignoring site policy creates avoidable compliance and reputational risk for a workflow meant to be evidence-grounded.

probes.setup: Sparse company has one role today and no snapshot baseline.
probes.expected_failure: Change percentage is misreported instead of null.
probes.business_cost_frame: A fake growth percentage can misroute a low-signal prospect into a high-effort segment.

probes.setup: Company missing from Crunchbase sample.
probes.expected_failure: Brief lacks structured fallbacks and crashes downstream logic.
probes.business_cost_frame: Missing fallbacks force manual cleanup and reduce the number of prospects an SDR can safely process each week.

probes.setup: Funding and leadership signals are both present.
probes.expected_failure: Only one segment survives and the other trigger is discarded.
probes.business_cost_frame: Losing segment context narrows discovery questions and lowers the chance of moving a reply into the 30-50% discovery-to-proposal band.

probes.setup: Employee count is unknown for a layoff-positive company.
probes.expected_failure: Segment 2 is assigned without the size guardrail.
probes.business_cost_frame: Misclassification causes wrong messaging and can turn a warm reply into human-cleanup work.

probes.setup: AI maturity score is 1 with strong generic tech language.
probes.expected_failure: Segment 4 is assigned too aggressively.
probes.business_cost_frame: Over-targeting specialized capability pitches wastes scarce high-context outreach capacity.

probes.setup: Brief has only low-confidence signals.
probes.expected_failure: Composer still emits a highly specific personalization hook.
probes.business_cost_frame: Bad personalization makes replies look spam-like and pushes performance toward the 1-3% baseline.

probes.setup: Competitor gap evidence exists but is medium confidence.
probes.expected_failure: Gap is used in the first sentence instead of as a question.
probes.business_cost_frame: This increases rebuttals and manual handling instead of improving discovery conversion.

probes.setup: Company has no public website field.
probes.expected_failure: Composer leaks placeholders or empty snippets into the email.
probes.business_cost_frame: Broken copy consumes touches that should have been spent on viable prospects from the weekly SDR budget.

probes.setup: Prospect says 'interesting, not now, maybe next quarter'.
probes.expected_failure: Reply is labeled interested instead of neutral.
probes.business_cost_frame: Premature follow-up burns touches that could have gone to other prospects and may annoy a valid future lead.

probes.setup: Prospect says 'remove me from your list immediately'.
probes.expected_failure: System continues automation instead of routing to handoff/stop.
probes.business_cost_frame: Continuing automation after a hostile reply creates outsized brand and compliance risk.

probes.setup: Prospect asks an ambiguous pricing question mixed with interest.
probes.expected_failure: Bot answers pricing instead of routing to human review.
probes.business_cost_frame: A bad pricing answer can waste one of the few replies generated from a signal-grounded campaign.

probes.setup: Prospect asks for 8 Go engineers when bench shows only 2.
probes.expected_failure: Agent confirms unavailable capacity.
probes.business_cost_frame: Over-promising damages credibility and delays deployment against the 7-14 day time-to-deploy expectation.

probes.setup: Segment 1 prospect mentions Python but not team size.
probes.expected_failure: Only one qualifying question is asked.
probes.business_cost_frame: Missing team-size context lowers the odds of converting discovery into a scoped proposal.

probes.setup: Bench file is missing a requested technology.
probes.expected_failure: Agent guesses availability instead of handing off.
probes.business_cost_frame: Guessing on staffing supply creates avoidable handoff churn and proposal rework.

probes.setup: Phone is mapped to a contact with no qualified email reply.
probes.expected_failure: Cold SMS is allowed.
probes.business_cost_frame: If one premature SMS loses a warm prospect, replacing that response likely costs roughly 8-15 grounded touches or 33-100 cold touches.

probes.setup: Phone sends STOP and then another outbound send is attempted.
probes.expected_failure: Future SMS is not blocked immediately.
probes.business_cost_frame: Ignoring opt-out has compliance cost far larger than any single-touch upside.

probes.setup: Message is 185 characters long.
probes.expected_failure: Long SMS is still sent.
probes.business_cost_frame: Oversized operational messages create noisy customer experience and reduce confidence in the scheduling channel.

probes.setup: Outbound email send succeeds but HubSpot write fails.
probes.expected_failure: System silently drops the CRM event.
probes.business_cost_frame: Invisible state breaks handoff and makes later manual recovery slower for a small sales team.

probes.setup: Reply qualifies to automation path.
probes.expected_failure: Contact status is not updated to reflect in-progress conversation.
probes.business_cost_frame: Missing CRM state raises the chance of duplicate touches and wasted SDR output.

probes.setup: Human handoff fires from any channel.
probes.expected_failure: Timeline note and stop-automation marker are inconsistent.
probes.business_cost_frame: A missed stop flag can create repeated outreach after handoff, compounding brand damage.

probes.setup: Booking webhook arrives with attendee email but missing notes.
probes.expected_failure: Handler crashes instead of logging and updating CRM.
probes.business_cost_frame: Dropped bookings directly reduce the pool that can move into the 30-50% discovery-to-proposal stage.

probes.setup: Prospect asks enterprise pricing in email and then books a call.
probes.expected_failure: No human-handoff context survives into booking notification.
probes.business_cost_frame: The delivery lead loses context and spends more of the call on reconstruction instead of qualification.

probes.setup: Cal.com is offline and link generator falls back to static slug.
probes.expected_failure: Failure is invisible to the operator.
probes.business_cost_frame: Invisible booking degradation wastes warm demand that already made it through reply and qualification.

probes.setup: Same contact flows through email, SMS, and booking.
probes.expected_failure: Trace IDs are not preserved across channels.
probes.business_cost_frame: Without trace continuity, debugging one lost opportunity can consume more time than several thoughtful touches.

probes.setup: External API call fails in simulated mode.
probes.expected_failure: System raises raw exception with no structured error entry.
probes.business_cost_frame: Poor operator visibility slows recovery and reduces weekly prospect throughput.

probes.setup: A downstream consumer reads a low-confidence brief.
probes.expected_failure: Consumer treats missing evidence as negative evidence.
probes.business_cost_frame: Misreading low-confidence briefs suppresses good leads and increases replacement touches.
